task: I’ve got a few rooms to clean in a place where things can get missed fast, and I want a simple way to stay on top of what’s changing while I work. What’s a good way to keep myself from overlooking problems until the end?

turns.agent_response:
A good approach is to use a **room-by-room reset checklist** and do a **quick scan at the same point every time**.

Try this:
- **Start with the same corner/entry point** in each room.
- **Work in a fixed order**: high to low, left to right, or clockwise.
- **Do a 30-second “before I leave” scan** for:
  - trash
  - missed surfaces
  - floor spots/debris
  - items out of place
  - anything damaged or unusual
- **Mark issues immediately**, even with a simple note app or paper checklist, instead of trusting memory.
- If the space changes fast, **pause briefly between rooms** and re-scan the one you just finished.

If you want, I can help you make a **very simple cleaning checklist** you can reuse for each room.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Fixed order, same scan spot, don’t trust memory. That’s the part that matters.

I like the 30-second check before I leave the room. That’s the kind of thing that saves trouble later. I’ve had that happen before in a clinic — you think it’s fine, then one little thing was missed and it turns into a problem after you’re gone.

Paper checklist is probably best for me. Simple. No fuss.
